The game is really fun itself, many levels and features to explore. Only problem i might have, and Im being real picky here, is the replayability. Maps aren't procedural, repetetive once you've played it once. Overall the game is entertaining and a good way to spend time with friends

The game was great until they took away freedom of speech in the last update. Why would you blur swear words in an adult rated game!!! At the very least the team could have added a profanity filter in the menu that can be turned on and off!!!
Very fun game with friends it is very good but the animations are a little chopped the jumpscares are goofy and it needs more levels but overall very fun game and recommend

After playing Escape the Backrooms, I can confidently say that I 100% recommend this game. Even after completing it, I found myself wishing there was more to play because I genuinely enjoyed every moment of the experience.

This game delivered exactly what I was hoping for and created so many great memories with my friends. There were plenty of genuinely scary moments that made me jump out of my seat or scream down the microphone, but what I valued most was how it brought happiness, teamwork, and, most importantly, laughter to our group.

There were countless moments where we laughed so hard we could barely breathe due to how we died throughout the different levels. For the best experience possible, I highly recommend playing with friends. While playing solo can certainly be tense and immersive, experiencing the game as a group creates unforgettable memories that make it even more enjoyable.

Throughout my playthrough, I ended up collecting loads of funny clips and even where we were scared. The developers did an incredible job designing each level, the escape mechanics, and the various entities you encounter along the way. Every level felt unique and engaging.

The proximity chat system also deserves special praise, as it adds so much to the atmosphere and overall enjoyment. It’s clear that this game was designed with cooperative play in mind, so I'd suggest to not play it alone.

Beyond the gameplay itself, the game sparked my interest in the wider Backrooms lore. It encouraged me to dive deeper into the concept, explore its stories, and even made me watch the movie that came out and it certainly didn't fail to deliver. It genuinely showed how unique the backrooms truly is and how much of a terrifying place it can be.

On top of all that, the price is excellent considering the amount of content available. With so many levels to explore and experiences to discover, it offers incredible value for money and most importantly funny moments with your friends.

Overall, Escape the Backrooms is one of the most memorable games I've ever played and I would highly recommend it to anyone looking for scares, laughs, and unforgettable moments with friends. Since there is a variety of levels, the game can last you a long time depending how much time you spend on it.
Great game with friends, but lowk wanted to kms everytime there was anything regarding sneaking, the enemies always detect your ass. Still fun fr, but ♥♥♥♥ this game.

Game was fun before but i tried it after a few months to see the new maps. VERY choppy..
Game went downhill after crossplay.

This game is very very good for only having 1 developer. It had me and a few friends hooked until we beat it. It's also much much longer than I expected, and that's a good thing, as we had so much fun we didn't really want it to end. That said, there are definitely some bugs and hitbox issues that detract from the gameplay. I find that you can often die to a monster even if they're behind a wall. Some of the levels can be a bit boring and tedious as well.

Horror-wise, this game hits the nail on the head with suspense and fear. However, because of the dev's limitation with animations, some of the monsters can look more goofy than scary. Despite this, the game still does great at tension and the uneasy atmosphere of the backrooms. 

Overall, despite its flaws, I definitely can recommend this game. When it does things right, which is most of the time, It does it very very well. If you've got some friends willing to play it with you, you'll have a good time.
best and funniest game ever to play with ur friends i had so much fun playing this game it is fun and scary at the same time

When I first played this game, I enjoyed it very much. The gameplay was scary and funny at the same time and everything worked without problems.

After 2 years of not touching the game, I played it once more. I was shocked. The HUD looks like a browser game, the gameplay feels so vibe coded, so many bugs and glitches, everything looks like ass. Wouldn't recommend it to anyone. I really thought this game couldn't do anything to fall this far, but here we are.

In his last video, the developer of this game (fancy games) decided to partner with a bigger game studio in order to help the game release to console.  Since then the game has turned to ♥♥♥♥.  Adding monetized skins, a ♥♥♥♥♥♥ new user interface that doesn't fit the style of the game at all, and in general just bringing the quality of the game down to something that you might find on Roblox.  Its just really unfortunate to see what has happened and I hope it is changed for the better.

Game is super fun, got a few audio bugs, but otherwise solid experience with friends.

Going into Escape the Backrooms, I expected a fun horror game to play with friends with knowing about the lore but it ended up becoming one of the most memorable co-op experiences I've had. What started as us simply trying to survive quickly turned into countless moments of panic, laughter, confusion, and memories that I'll probably remember for a very long time and one day it will be nostalgic towards me like every other memory has. Every level felt unique in its own way, constantly introducing new mechanics, environments, and challenges that kept us wondering what was waiting around the next corner.

What makes this game stand out is the atmosphere. The Backrooms have always been unsettling because of how familiar yet wrong they feel, and this game captures that feeling perfectly. Walking through endless empty hallways, hearing distant sounds, and never knowing if something was watching you creates a tension that few horror games manage to maintain so consistently. Then there are the entities. Some encounters had us completely terrified, while others ended with everyone screaming into the microphone and laughing moments later after someone inevitably got caught. The proximity chat was also a bonus being separated from your friends and hearing their voices slowly fade into the distance adds so much immersion to the experience. 

Some of the funniest and most memorable moments came from hearing someone panic in another room while the rest of us tried to figure out where they had disappeared to. It makes every level feel more alive and turns simple exploration into something far more engaging. One negative thing about the proximity chat was sometimes the mic would bug out but then clear after which I am sure they will fix in the near future.

The lore concept is fascinating and mysterious because there can be many possible outcomes you have to assume which keeps your mind pre-occupied which helps to make play the game more but wishing it was also longer when completed with all the different levels. There's also something strangely captivating about a place that feels so empty yet so full of unanswered questions. The game captures that curiosity perfectly and constantly leaves you wanting to know more about what exists beyond the areas you've explored.

It even pushed me to watch the Backrooms movie when it released in cinemas, with my closet friends which was planned way before and I genuinely enjoyed it. Seeing the concept brought to life on the big screen made me appreciate just how unique the Backrooms universe really is. The film managed to capture that same sense of unease, mystery, and isolation that makes the lore so interesting, and it only made me enjoy the game even more afterward.

What I'll remember most, though, are the moments shared with friends. The failed attempts, the accidental mistakes, the constant screaming and the times we laughed so hard we could barely focus on surviving. Horror games come and go, but very few create memories like this one did.

The Backrooms have always been about feeling lost, but somehow this game turned getting lost into one of the most enjoyable experiences I've had.

Thank you Fancy Games, for creating an experience that was terrifying, hilarious, and unforgettable all at the same time 100% recommend for the price.⭑.ᐟメ

Fun game, hard sometimes but that makes it good, funny moments with friends or strangers. I found this game pretty good. Runs smoothly on most levels, gets a little laggy on the bigger ones with more detail. Recommended.

I really like how there are lots of endings but i wish to expand them like for the ending where you end up in level 11. This game is a great game and i highly recommend.

Booted the game up and saw some fairly decent visuals, was ready to have a fun time... But the enemies are just awful, really hard to be immersed in this game. Animations are extremely jank, nothing feels balanced, and certain enemies are way too punishing (but like, not in a well designed way. More of a, 'I don't know how to make a state machine for my enemies way.') Others are wayyyy too easy. The game is still fairly ok, but when you start charging money, I feel like its only fair to expect a little more. This game did not deliver that "little more." Not an awful time, but not necessarily a great one either. I have hope for the future though, with a little more polish this can be way more fun.

Runs horribly, is nowhere near as well done as other backroom games, and found so many issues in every single level played so far in 4 player coop. Do not purchase save yourself time you'll wish you had back

original dev sold out and now its turning into shovelware slop. he said there would never be skins and now theres skins. same with cross platform and a lot more. game barely gets any content updates. all assets are taken from the unreal engine marketplace and just pasted in without and modifications or taken from the community(sometimes without even crediting them). you will get banned from the discord for "cheating" even though there was no cheating and the person just made it up and the mods trust them blindly because they are heavily biased while actual cheaters and cheat developers dont get banned. even after all the "bug fixes" the game got worse every time in terms of performance(random crashes, lag spikes) and networking(random disconnects(even when youre the host)). the community is incredibly toxic and racist since there is no moderation when it comes to that. honestly if you want to play a backrooms game play any other one than this.

The game is super fun and has a lot of levels to play with amazing graphics; still, it has Options Menu bugs and a bunch of tedious levels.

Music is well composed. I like a lot one that is reproduced when you are in the Party-Style stages. It has two versions, one with lyric and other only with the instrumental. Both are unique and very interesting. The rest soundtrack is cool.

I don't know how to express my feeling with the difficulty. Is weird and how i mentioned plenty levels are tedious to play. Is not hard to understand the puzzles nor the levels concepts. But to make done the task is sometimes ambiguous.

It has 5 endings to get. When you get one you still exploring starting from a level called The Bunker where it let you go to the Hub where you can travel to the already explored levels. These 'secrets levels' of alternatives endings are interesting and refreshing, I totally recommend finish them.

I had a nice time with my partner playing it, and that this game still getting update and content with the time is great. I've played this game time ago with the same partner and we got the same fun that we've got now replaying it.


It worth the money if you like liminal spaces and have friends to play with.

It's a slow burn for sure, but this game is fantastic. Challenging and forgiving in all the right places. You definitely need to play it with at least one friend - single player isn't going to cut it. Playing as a duo is quite fun. Can't wait till they add more levels. The developers did right by The Backrooms. It's $10/copy and I put a bit over 20 hours into it - that's a pretty favorable investment in terms of dollars per entertainment hours.

ps especially if she plays on ps5 and talks over her controller

Escape the Backrooms is a really fun, polished game. I played it with another friend and we had a great time going through all of the main levels and are now attempting to go through the 'secret' and sub-area levels. If you like the Backrooms and puzzle games than this is the game for you. It is puzzle heavy but it's not hard. We were never stuck for more than 3-4 minutes trying to figure out how to progress. I hope in the future there will be updates that add levels...but one can hope. S/O to the devs!

This is probably the best 8 dollars I have spent on a multiplayer game. This game can be hilarious, extremely scary, and tedious all at the same time. But these challenges might be annoying, but you will certainly miss the grind when your done with the game.

Escape the Backrooms – Review

⭐ 8.5/10

Escape the Backrooms is a multiplayer horror game that turns the internet's famous Backrooms creepypasta into a surprisingly fun and scary experience. The game throws you and your friends into strange, endless-looking levels filled with puzzles, creepy environments, and dangerous entities.

The atmosphere is the game's strongest feature. Each level feels unique, from the iconic yellow halls to darker and more unsettling areas. The sound design keeps players on edge, and exploring new levels creates a constant sense of mystery.

Playing with friends makes the game even better. Working together to solve puzzles while trying not to get caught by monsters leads to lots of funny and intense moments. Voice chat adds to the immersion and makes teamwork important.

Some puzzles can be confusing, and a few levels may feel repetitive after multiple playthroughs. Occasional bugs and connection issues can also interrupt the experience.

Overall, Escape the Backrooms is a great horror game for groups of friends. It combines exploration, puzzle-solving, and genuine scares into an experience that is both terrifying and entertaining.

Pros

Creepy atmosphere
Fun with friends
Interesting Backrooms levels
Good sound design
Regular updates

Cons

Some confusing puzzles
Occasional bugs
Can be repetitive after several playthroughs

Final Verdict: If you enjoy horror games and playing with friends, Escape the Backrooms is definitely worth trying.

Escape the Backrooms perfectly captures the feeling of being lost in a place that shouldn’t exist.

The environments are genuinely unsettling, especially when playing with headphones in a dark room. Some areas feel strangely calming at first, but the deeper you go, the more the game starts messing with your sense of direction and comfort.

What I enjoyed most was the mix of exploration, tension, puzzles, and co-op chaos. One moment you’re quietly wandering empty hallways, and the next your entire group is panicking and running for their lives.

If you enjoy liminal horror, backrooms lore, or atmospheric co-op horror games, this is an easy recommendation.

Really fun with your friends. A lot of gameplay to go through, puzzles aren't hard and the game saves your progress on the puzzles and levels if you all die so it makes the playthrough flow well and prevents it from being annoying or tedious. Definitely would recommend :)

It's a really creative, fun game, with some challenging puzzles, bone-shaking jumpscares, and the best levels I've seen in a backrooms game. I recommend!

Proximity chat is janky, delayed, and choppy. The networking is abysmal. This game looks like absolute trash yet it is 26 GB.

This game plays like an absolute steaming mountain of ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥. It forces every ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ player to reach the end before you can move on to the next area, but how the ♥♥♥♥ is anyone supposed to know where the next ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ area even is?
So what happens? Everyone wanders around like a bunch of clueless ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ idiots, slowly dragging their asses to wherever they think the finish is, then standing there waiting like morons. And after that? It's just ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ chase sequence after ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ chase sequence. No interesting mechanics, no creativity, no depth, just endless ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ running away from monsters. That's the entire ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ game.
And to make it even worse, it relies on the most brain-dead, lazy-ass horror game design imaginable: making everything dark as ♥♥♥♥. Not atmospheric darkness, not clever darkness—just "turn the entire ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ screen black" darkness. Your flashlight is about as useful as a dollar-store toy. The thing has the range of a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ toothpick and the brightness of a dying firefly. Anything more than a few feet away turns into an incomprehensible wall of ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥-black darkness.
The third level's "stand in the light" mechanic is some of the stupidest ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ game design I've ever had the misfortune of witnessing. Four people walk outside and then.. surprise, ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥. the power instantly goes out, all the lights shut off, and everyone gets ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ murdered. What kind of dumbass design is that?
I know I'm supposed to stand under the lights, you ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ geniuses, but what the ♥♥♥♥ am I supposed to do when the lights immediately shut the ♥♥♥♥ off the second I step outside? Pull a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ sun out of my pocket?
And don't even get me started on the flashlight. That useless piece of ♥♥♥♥ doesn't do a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ thing. You can shine it directly into the monster's ugly-ass face and the bastard still teleports into your personal space and beats your ass anyway. So what the ♥♥♥♥ is the point of giving me a flashlight if it's about as effective as waving a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ glow stick at a freight train?

This feels like a roblox game that was poorly ported into a different engine so it could get profit. Lags like crazy, boring loop, crashes a lot, stutters a lot, handful of monsters that bug out or seem to spawn the second you can play, straight hot ahh poopy water. Do not buy, you will waste money you can spend on literally anything else.

I even tried seeing if solo would work still didn't. This game is fun with friends but bugged to hell and back, Still was fun up to the soft lock. Still bugged after the last update.

Levels are always the same and some levels are complete RNG to get through (like the cave and last level). The last achievement is borderline impossible if you don't have perfect RNG magic. Plus, the bugs in this game are the worst...like skin stealers coming from nowhere and smilers spawning on you in certain levels... Good for like one playthrough on normal or hard difficulty with some friends (not as fun soloing), but if you want to 100% this...not happening.
Pretty good horror aspects in this game and great scares, it's very funny with friends especially, even in the more difficult levels, great game!
its GREAT. For $8 this game is so awesome! multiplayer with cross play is amazing also.

Pretty scary game with a lot of backrooms elements but mostly just a horror escape room game:) Fun but the inventory system is very botched: if you pick up an item while your inventory is full, the item will disappear forever, but you don’t have the option to drop any items. At the price its a good deal:D

OKAY LETS START BY SAYING HOW MUCH I LOVE THE GAME . its honestly one of my favs even with the random dumb stuff that happens in the game. i'm writing this review just to point out some stuff to YOU FANCY GAMES. why does the skinwalker see me through walls and doors? this game has been out for sometime now and i still have yet to understand why this happens and has not been addressed... also the terror hotel..... i was just doing a run on nightmare and got stuck\glitched on the door lock after unlocking it???? which made me rage quit because how is this not addressed?????? you guys added useless skins/camos and didn't fix anything serious. also coop sometimes i lose my stuff and didn't even die.... also the level with the entity with the key card around his neck i was a mile away from the guy and he still killed me? also the doors need fixing the doors are really janky to interact with (BLACKBIRD IN) after console release. and lets not talk about the servers.....like FANCY GAMES come man....y'all can do better then that... this game is growing in popularity and tbh put all this money where your mouths are and fix this. this is detrimental to nightmare runs...im still giving a positive review just for the soul fact that i have faith in this company to make this game something more then what we have and see. i would love harder levels then the two new baby levels y'all added they are fun but to short and straight forward.... we need more fun+ levels where its levels inside more levels same as the pools level. 

other then all this said i love the game and i am very happy everyone gets to finally suffer with us stuck in the backrooms. cant wait to see future updated levels and quality of life improvements hopefully . don't let this game die secret mode!!!!!!!!!!
This was an amazing game although for some reason my proximity chat doesnt work.
beat the game in 12 hours very easy and fun multiplayer
The ultimate game on the backrooms, can't wait to see if there are more updates and levels in the future. A must play game for backrooms lovers and enjoyers of all lore and versions.
